title: The Case of the Missing Lucky Penny
synopsis: When Kofi's best friend Maya loses her lucky penny before the big spelling bee, he puts on his detective hat to solve the mystery. With clues scattered around the school and suspects everywhere, Kofi must use his observation skills and the help of his friends to crack the case before it's too late.

chapters.title: The Missing Lucky Penny
chapters.content:
Kofi was eating his peanut butter sandwich when Maya rushed over to his lunch table. Her face looked worried, and her hands were shaking.

"Kofi, I need your help!" Maya said, sitting down quickly. "My lucky penny is missing!"

Kofi put down his sandwich and looked at his best friend. Maya always carried her lucky penny everywhere. It was shiny and old, and she rubbed it before every test and spelling bee.

"When did you last see it?" Kofi asked, pulling out a small notebook from his backpack. He had been reading detective books lately and always kept notes.

"This morning before school started," Maya said. "I put it in my desk like I always do. But when I went to get it for the spelling bee practice, it was gone!"

The spelling bee was tomorrow, and Maya had been practicing for weeks. She was one of the best spellers in third grade, but she always said her lucky penny helped her feel brave.

"Don't worry, Maya," Kofi said, standing up. "Detective Kofi is on the case! We'll find your penny before tomorrow."

Maya smiled for the first time since she sat down. "Really? You think we can find it?"

"I know we can," Kofi said confidently. "But first, we need to go back to your classroom and look for clues."

illustrations.description: Kofi, a boy with short cropped hair, rich dark brown skin, bright expressive eyes, sits at a lunch table with a notebook open, while Maya sits across from him looking worried. The scene is lit with dramatic shadows and a spotlight effect, giving it a film noir for kids atmosphere with muted colors and one yellow accent color from the school cafeteria lights.
illustrations.alt: Kofi taking notes while Maya explains her missing penny problem

chapters.title: Searching for Clues
chapters.content:
After lunch, Kofi and Maya walked back to Maya's classroom. The hallway was quiet since most students were still eating. Their footsteps echoed as they walked.

"Show me exactly where you kept the penny," Kofi said, taking out a magnifying glass he had borrowed from the science room.

Maya led him to her desk in the third row. She opened the small compartment where she kept her special things.

"Right here," she said, pointing to an empty spot. "Next to my eraser and my purple pen."

Kofi looked carefully with his magnifying glass. The desk was neat and clean, but he noticed something interesting.

"Look at this," he said, pointing to some small scratches on the wood. "These look fresh. Like someone was looking around in here."

Maya leaned in closer. "You're right! Those weren't there yesterday."

Kofi wrote in his notebook: "Fresh scratches on desk. Someone searched through Maya's things."

Then he noticed something else. On the floor near Maya's desk was a small piece of blue paper.

"What's this?" Kofi said, picking it up carefully.

It was torn, but they could see part of a word written on it: "LUC..."

"LUC... that could be the start of 'LUCKY,'" Maya said excitedly.

"Our first real clue," Kofi said, putting the paper in a small envelope he kept for evidence. "Now we need to figure out who might have dropped this."

illustrations.description: Kofi, a boy with short cropped hair, rich dark brown skin, bright expressive eyes, examines Maya's desk with a magnifying glass while dramatic shadows fall across the empty classroom. The scene has a detective atmosphere with muted colors and one blue accent color from the torn paper clue, creating a film noir for kids mood.
illustrations.alt: Kofi investigating Maya's desk with a magnifying glass while looking for clues

chapters.title: The List of Suspects
chapters.content:
Kofi and Maya sat on the playground swings, thinking about who might have taken the penny. Kofi had his notebook open and was making a list.

"Let's think about who knew about your lucky penny," Kofi said.

"Well, everyone in our class knows about it," Maya said. "I always rub it before spelling tests."

"But who would want to take it?" Kofi asked.

Maya thought for a moment. "Maybe someone who's also in the spelling bee tomorrow?"

Kofi wrote down three names: "Jake, Emma, and Sam."

Jake sat near Maya and was always looking at other people's things. Emma was also in the spelling bee and seemed nervous about it. Sam had been acting strange lately, avoiding Maya and Kofi.

"We need to talk to each of them," Kofi said. "But we have to be careful. We can't let them know we suspect them."

Just then, they saw Jake walking by the swings. He was carrying his backpack and looking around nervously.

"There's Jake now," Maya whispered.

"Follow me," Kofi said quietly. "Let's see what he's up to."

They got off the swings and followed Jake at a distance. He went behind the school building where the old bike racks were. Very few students went back there.

"Why is he going back there?" Maya asked.

"I don't know," Kofi said, "but we're about to find out."

They crept closer, staying hidden behind the corner of the building.

illustrations.description: Kofi, a boy with short cropped hair, rich dark brown skin, bright expressive eyes, sits on a swing with his notebook open while Maya sits on the swing next to him. The playground scene is filled with dramatic shadows and spotlights filtering through trees, creating a mysterious film noir for kids atmosphere with muted colors and one red accent color from the swing set.
illustrations.alt: Kofi and Maya on swings discussing their list of suspects

chapters.title: Following Jake
chapters.content:
Kofi and Maya peeked around the corner of the building. Jake was kneeling by the old bike racks, digging through his backpack.

"What's he looking for?" Maya whispered.

Kofi held up his hand for quiet and watched carefully. Jake pulled out something small and shiny from his backpack.

"Is that my penny?" Maya gasped.

But when Jake held it up to the light, they could see it was just a regular quarter.

"False alarm," Kofi said softly.

Jake put the quarter back in his backpack and walked away. He still looked nervous, but he wasn't their penny thief.

"Cross Jake off the list," Kofi said, making a note in his notebook.

They walked back toward the main playground and saw Emma sitting alone by the monkey bars. She was reading a book and looked very focused.

"Let's go talk to Emma," Kofi suggested.

They walked over casually, trying to look like they just wanted to chat.

"Hi Emma," Maya said. "What are you reading?"

Emma looked up. "Oh, hi! I'm reading about spelling bee champions. I'm so nervous about tomorrow."

"Yeah, me too," Maya said. "I just wish I had something to help me feel more confident."

Kofi watched Emma's face carefully. She looked genuinely confused.

"Like what?" Emma asked.

"Oh, just something lucky, you know?" Maya said.

Emma's face lit up. "I know what you mean! I have my lucky bracelet." She held up her wrist, showing a simple silver bracelet. "My grandmother gave it to me."

Kofi could tell Emma wasn't their thief either. She was too honest and open about her own lucky charm.

illustrations.description: Kofi, a boy with short cropped hair, rich dark brown skin, bright expressive eyes, and Maya peek around a corner watching Jake at the old bike racks. The scene is shot with dramatic film noir lighting creating long shadows and spotlights, with muted colors and one silver accent color from the quarter Jake is holding, maintaining a mysterious but not scary detective atmosphere for kids.
illustrations.alt: Kofi and Maya secretly watching Jake from behind a building corner

chapters.title: The Real Culprit
chapters.content:
That left Sam as their last suspect. Kofi and Maya looked around the playground but couldn't find him anywhere.

"Where could Sam be?" Maya wondered.

"Let's check the library," Kofi suggested. "He likes to read there sometimes."

They walked into the quiet library and looked around. Mrs. Peterson, the librarian, pointed toward the back corner where Sam was sitting alone at a small table.

As they got closer, they could see Sam had his head in his hands. He looked upset about something.

"Sam?" Maya said gently. "Are you okay?"

Sam looked up, and his eyes were red like he had been crying.

"I'm sorry, Maya," Sam said quietly. "I took your lucky penny."

Maya gasped, but Kofi put a gentle hand on her arm.

"Why did you take it, Sam?" Kofi asked kindly.

Sam reached into his pocket and pulled out Maya's shiny penny. "I'm in the spelling bee too, and I've been so scared. I thought if I borrowed your lucky penny, it might help me not be so nervous."

"But Sam," Maya said, sitting down next to him, "you're a great speller! You don't need a lucky penny."

"I was going to put it back after the spelling bee," Sam continued. "I just wanted to borrow it. But then I felt so bad about taking it without asking."

Kofi sat down too. "That's why you've been avoiding us, isn't it?"

Sam nodded. "I felt terrible. I'm really sorry, Maya."

Maya took her penny back, but she looked thoughtful.

illustrations.description: Kofi, a boy with short cropped hair, rich dark brown skin, bright expressive eyes, sits at a library table with Maya and Sam, who is holding the lucky penny. The library scene features dramatic shadows between the bookshelves with soft spotlights, creating a film noir for kids atmosphere with muted colors and one copper accent color from the penny, maintaining a mysterious but comforting mood.
illustrations.alt: Kofi, Maya and Sam having a serious conversation in the library corner

chapters.title: Making Things Right
chapters.content:
"Sam," Maya said, "I understand why you took it. Being nervous about the spelling bee is really scary."

Sam wiped his eyes. "I know I shouldn't have taken it without asking. I just didn't know how to tell you I was scared."

Kofi had an idea. "What if we all helped each other get ready for the spelling bee?"

"How?" Sam asked.

"We could practice together," Maya suggested. "And Sam, you know what? My lucky penny doesn't really make me a better speller. It just helps me feel brave. But I think having good friends helps even more."

Sam looked surprised. "Really?"

"Really," Maya said. "Want to know a secret? I've been practicing really hard for weeks. The penny just reminds me that I'm prepared."

Kofi nodded. "And Sam, you've won classroom spelling contests before. You're already a great speller."

Sam started to smile a little. "I guess I forgot about that."

"How about this," Maya said. "Tomorrow before the spelling bee, we'll all sit together and remind each other how prepared we are. That's even better than lucky charms."

"And Sam," Kofi added, "next time you're worried about something, just talk to us. We're your friends. We want to help."

Sam nodded. "I promise I will. And I'm really sorry I took your penny without asking."

"I forgive you," Maya said, giving Sam a hug. "But next time, just ask if you need to borrow something!"

All three friends laughed together.

illustrations.description: Kofi, a boy with short cropped hair, rich dark brown skin, bright expressive eyes, stands with Maya and Sam in a group hug in the library. Warm spotlights filter down through the library windows creating a film noir for kids atmosphere with dramatic but gentle shadows, muted colors and one warm golden accent color, showing the mysterious mood lifting into friendship and forgiveness.
illustrations.alt: The three friends sharing a group hug in the library

chapters.title: The Spelling Bee Victory
chapters.content:
The next morning, Kofi sat in the school auditorium watching the spelling bee. Maya, Sam, and Emma were all on stage with the other contestants.

Before the bee started, Kofi had seen his three friends sitting together, encouraging each other and practicing words. Maya had her lucky penny in her pocket, but she looked confident even without touching it.

The spelling bee began. One by one, students spelled their words. Some got them right, others missed and had to sit down.

Emma spelled "butterfly" perfectly and smiled at her friends. Sam nailed "adventure" without hesitating. Maya got "mystery" right and winked at Kofi in the audience.

As the bee continued, fewer students remained on stage. Emma missed "philosophy" but she smiled and waved to her friends as she sat down.

"You did great!" Maya whispered as Emma walked by.

Now only Sam and Maya were left, along with one other student.

Sam got "telescope" correct and looked surprised at how confident he felt. Maya spelled "magnificent" without any trouble.

In the final round, it came down to just Maya and Sam. They looked at each other and smiled.

Sam got "encyclop​edia" but he mixed up two letters. He looked disappointed for a moment, then remembered what his friends had told him about being proud of how far he'd come.

Maya stepped up for the winning word: "friendship."

She spelled it perfectly: "F-R-I-E-N-D-S-H-I-P."

The audience cheered! Maya had won, but the first thing she did was hug Sam.

"You were amazing," she told him.

Kofi ran up on stage to congratulate both his friends. "Case closed," he said with a grin. "And the real treasure wasn't the lucky penny after all."

"What was it?" Sam asked.

"Having friends who believe in you," Kofi said.

Maya pulled out her lucky penny and looked at it. "You know what? I think this penny is luckiest when I share it with my friends."

From that day on, whenever any of them faced something scary, they would pass the lucky penny around. But they all knew the real magic was in supporting each other.

illustrations.description: Kofi, a boy with short cropped hair, rich dark brown skin, bright expressive eyes, stands on stage with Maya and Sam under bright spotlights in the school auditorium. The scene captures the film noir for kids atmosphere with dramatic stage lighting and shadows, muted colors with one bright golden accent color from the stage lights, showing the triumphant but mysterious mood of solving the case and winning the spelling bee.
illustrations.alt: Kofi celebrating with Maya and Sam on the spelling bee stage

questions.question: What was missing from Maya's desk?
questions.explanation: Maya's lucky penny was missing from her desk, which she always kept there and needed for confidence during the spelling bee.

questions.question: What clue did Kofi find on the floor near Maya's desk?
choices: A torn piece of blue paper with 'LUC...'
questions.explanation: Kofi found a torn piece of blue paper with part of the word 'LUC...' on it, which they thought might be from 'LUCKY'.

questions.question: Who were the three suspects on Kofi's list?
questions.explanation: Kofi wrote down Jake, Emma, and Sam as suspects because they all knew about Maya's lucky penny and were in the spelling bee.

questions.question: Why did Sam take Maya's lucky penny?
choices: He was nervous about the spelling bee
He wanted to keep it forever
questions.explanation: Sam took the penny because he was scared and nervous about the spelling bee, hoping it would help him feel more confident.

questions.question: Where did Kofi and Maya find Sam?
questions.explanation: They found Sam in the library, sitting alone at a table in the back corner, looking upset.

questions.question: What was the final word Maya had to spell to win the spelling bee?
questions.explanation: The winning word was 'friendship,' which Maya spelled correctly: F-R-I-E-N-D-S-H-I-P.

questions.question: What did the friends learn was more important than lucky charms?
choices: Having friends who believe in you
questions.explanation: The friends learned that having supportive friends who believe in you is more valuable than any lucky charm.

Maya _____ Sam for taking her penny without asking.
